'''Joshua Charnley''' (born 26 June 1991) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] and the [[Wigan Warriors]], with whom he won the [[2013 Super League Grand Final|2013]] and [[2016 Super League Grand Final]]s, and was on loan from Wigan at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> At international level, he played for the [[England Knights]] in 2011 and won 8 caps for [[England national rugby league team|England]] between 2012 and 2014.

Charnley also played [[rugby union]] for the [[Sale Sharks]] in the [[Aviva Premiership]] from 2016 to 2018.

==Club career==
===Academy===
Charnley made numerous appearances for the Under 20s in 2010 before joining [[Blackpool Panthers]] on dual registration and then [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on loan. He was a player around the fringes of a first team opportunity at Wigan before the possibility came to join Hull Kingston Rovers for a month. Injuries meant he was given the chance to play first team rugby by Hull KR coach [[Justin Morgan (rugby league)|Justin Morgan]], making his [[Super League]] debut. Charnley showed great promise on his debut for Hull KR against [[Leeds Rhinos]] and picked up the club man of the match award. He scored five tries in five appearances,<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/18/josh-charnley-wigan-hull-kr|title=Josh Charnley recalled by Wigan from Hull KR loan spell|work=The Guardian|access-date=6 July 2023}}</ref> including a hat-trick in a home win over [[Castleford Tigers]], and the opening try in the derby clash with [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].

===Wigan Warriors===
Charnley made his first team debut for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]] against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in August where he scored a try off the bench.

He played on the wing in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] side at [[Wembley Stadium]]. Charnley scored the first try, and broke a bone in his hand in his side's victory over Leeds.<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18-28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18-28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}</ref>

[[File:Josh Charnley 2011.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Charnley playing for Wigan]]

In the 2012 Super League season he scored 31 tries to help Wigan to the [[League Leaders' Shield]]. This put Charnley in third place in the all-time top try scorers in a regular season in the summer era as only [[Lesley Vainikolo]] (36 tries) and [[Danny McGuire]] (35 tries), both in 2004, have scored more tries in a Super League regular season.

Charnley then went onto an even better 2013 season helping Wigan to the double (Challenge Cup winners and Super League champions) scoring a record-breaking 43 tries ranking him first. He played in the [[2013 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23794947|title= Hull FC 0-16 Wigan Warriors|date=24 August 2013|publisher= BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/aug/24/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final|title= Wigan Warriors overcome Hull FC and elements to win Challenge Cup|date=24 August 2013|work= The Guardian|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3660839,00.html|title= Wigan Warriors grind out victory over Hull|date=24 August 2013|publisher= Sky Sports|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref> He played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |access-date=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archive-date=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|publisher=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=The Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

Although the game ended in a 36–14 defeat for Wigan, Charnley scored two tries in the [[2014 World Club Challenge]] against NRL champions the [[Sydney Roosters]]. On 18 June 2014, he scored five tries in a 48–4 victory over [[Widnes Vikings]]. Despite missing two months of action due to a knee injury, Charnley still scored at a rate of over a try a game with 23 tries in 21 games in the 2014 season.

He played in the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=The Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref> During the 2016 season, it was announced that Charnley would switch codes to join [[Sale Sharks]] at the end of the 2016 Super League season. Charnley signed off in style as he scored the winning try in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], chasing and touching down a beautifully weighted kick from [[Dan Sarginson]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|work=The Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

===Sale Sharks===
On 30 March 2016, Charnley made the cross-code switch to [[rugby union]] with Manchester-based [[Sale Sharks]], who compete in the [[Aviva Premiership]], for the 2016–17 season.<ref>{{cite news|url= http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/sale-sharks-sign-wigan-warriors-11110528|publisher=Manchester Evening News|title=Sale Sharks sign Wigan Warriors star Josh Charnley|date=30 March 2016|access-date=11 April 2017}}</ref> Charnley was not able to repeat his scoring success in rugby union, bagging four tries in 32 games. Charnley then returned to rugby league for the 2018 summer season, his last game in rugby union coming against [[London Irish]] in February.

===Warrington Wolves===
Charnley returned to rugby league for the 2018 season with [[Super League]] club, [[Warrington Wolves]]. He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]]<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|work=The Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref> and the [[2018 Super League Grand Final|Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|work=The Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

In his second season with the Wolves, Charnley played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|work=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>

===Leigh Leopards===
On 25 June 2022, Charnley signed an initial loan deal with Championship side Leigh with a permanent contract starting in 2023.  On 10 July 2022, Charnley made his debut for Leigh, scoring a try during a 66–0 victory over [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/josh-charnley-makes-immediate-switch-to-leigh-centurions/|title=Josh Charnley makes immediate switch to Leigh|website=loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
On 28 August 2022, Charnley scored two tries for Leigh in a 42–4 victory over [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/championship-rl-results-and-round-up-leigh-centurions-take-huge-step-towards-super-league-as-halifax-panthers-batter-bradford-bulls-58193/|title=Championship RL results and round-up: Leigh Centurions take huge step towards Super League as Halifax Panthers batter Bradford Bulls|website=seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>
In round 3 of the 2023 Super League season, Charnley scored two tries including the winner as Leigh earned their first win back in the top flight defeating [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] 30-25.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/news/23362764.leigh-leopards-beat-hull-kr-first-super-league-win/|title=Leigh Leopards beat Hull KR for first Super League win|website=www.leighjournal.co.uk|date=4 March 2023 }}</ref>
In round 6, Charnley scored a hat-trick as Leigh defeated [[Hull F.C.]] 24-16.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65039973|title=Super League: Hull FC 16-24 Leigh - Josh Charnley runs in hat-trick as Leopards win again|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, Charnley played for Leigh in their 17-16 Challenge Cup final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
Charnley played 26 games for Leigh in the 2023 Super League season and scored 27 tries as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in their elimination playoff loss against Hull Kington Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./|title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review|website=www.leighleopards.co.uk|date=16 February 2024 }}</ref>
In May 2024, he scored his 300th career try in a win against [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Two-try Josh Charnley fires Leigh's play-off ambitions |url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/24313373.two-try-josh-charnley-fires-leighs-play-off-ambitions/ |website=Leigh Journal |access-date=5 June 2024 |date=11 May 2024}}</ref>
Charnley played 25 games for Leigh in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish fifth on the table. He played in the clubs semi-final loss to Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12204/13227859/super-league-semi-finals-live-wigan-face-leigh-for-grand-final-spot/|title=Super League semi-finals: Wigan Warriors cruise past Leigh Leopards- live commentary and video clips|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

==International career==
Charnley made his representative debut for the [[England Knights]] in a friendly against [[France national rugby league team|France]] in 2011, scoring two tries and kicking four goals from six kicks.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/9308666.england-knights-38-france-18/|title=England Knights 38 France 18|work=Warrington Guardian|access-date=6 July 2023}}</ref> He went on to make another scoring appearance against [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]] one week later.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/15355969|title=Cumbria 12-26 England Knights|work=BBC Sport|access-date=6 July 2023}}</ref>

Charnley made his senior international debut for [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in the [[2012 Autumn International Series]], scoring four tries in his first appearance for England.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/20042115|work=BBC Sport|title=Autumn International Series: England thrash Wales|access-date=6 July 2023}}</ref> He represented England in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]], scoring a try against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the tournament's opening fixture,<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24650021|title=England Lose World Cup Opener|work=BBC Sport|access-date=14 September 2017}}</ref> and a brace against France in the quarter-final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24964307|access-date=6 July 2023|work=BBC Sport|title=England reach semi-finals}}</ref> During the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]], he scored a try in the loss to [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/new-zealand-vs-england/report/41787|title=New Zealand edge England to cement final spot|work=Sky Sports|access-date=6 July 2023}}</ref>

==Honours==
'''[[Super League|Grand Final]]: 3'''
::[[Super League XV|2010]], [[Super League XVIII|2013]], [[Super League XXI|2016]]

'''[[Super League|League Leaders' Shield]]: 2'''
::[[Super League XV|2010]], [[Super League XVII|2012]]

'''[[Challenge Cup]]: 4'''
::[[2011 Challenge Cup|2011]], [[2013 Challenge Cup|2013]], [[2019 Challenge Cup|2019]], [[2023 Challenge Cup|2023]]

'''Super League Top Try Scorer: 2'''
::[[Super League XVII|2012]] (31 tries), [[Super League XVIII|2013]] (33 tries)

He is joint 21st with [[Garry Schofield]] on the all time record of 330 tries in the British game (318 at club level, 2 for England Knights & 10 for {{rlnt|England}}) (as of 17 May 2026)<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 May 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Josh Charnley climbs game’s all-time try-scorer list following latest effort|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/josh-charnley-try-standing-17-05-2026}}</ref>
